hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: Two-thirds of the crowd expects a DHS shutdown deal by end of Q1 2026, which prices in either institutional competence or belief that March deadlines force compromise better than September ones did. The real tell: volume spiked 10 points in 24 hours on zero new catalyst, suggesting someone with conviction just loaded up on YES, watch if Congressional leadership actually tables a proposal or if this is just momentum chasing.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Just Jumped 10 Points. Here's What Got Priced In.
daily_take.deck: The market swung 10.1 percentage points overnight on the DHS shutdown question, pushing odds to 66.4% that this ends after March 31. Something spooked traders into expecting a longer standoff. The real move isn't whether it resolves, it's whether you believe Congress actually caves before that deadline or if this drags past Q1. At 66%, you're getting paid for chaos. Smart money should hunt for what actually changed on the Hill.

sidebar.headline: Iran escalation odds stayed flat despite Middle East noise. This market is pricing in restraint, not action.
